content.text:
tomorrow, I'm part of an interview panel for this role:

📬 About Stable
Our mission is to make it simple to headquarter any business on the internet. Today, we provide companies with a business address and a dashboard to manage their physical mail online. Over 10,000+ companies like Gitlab, Glassdoor, and Indiegogo use Stable to automate their mailroom and act as their permanent business address with the IRS, state, and vendors.

The rules that regulate US entities were written in the 1800s. Stable abstracts these antiquated requirements with tools that empower modern companies to move forward faster.

These rules don't make sense for the way we work today — work takes place in the cloud and businesses are no longer tied to physical proximity or geography.

We’re on a mission to fix the broken system of entity management. Starting with business addresses and mail, we’re abstracting the complex, archaic systems that make company-building painful and turning them into delightful experiences — so that modern businesses have the tools they need to move forward faster.

We're backed by leading Silicon Valley investors like Y Combinator, Craft Ventures, Shakti, Hustle Fund, and founders from companies like Lattice, Apartment List, and FlexJobs.

Our business is at an inflection point. We’re growing quickly with a product people love and we’ve proven we can service companies of all stages and industries — from early-stage startups to publicly traded companies in industries like technology, logistics, and property management.

This is an opportunity to join an early-stage startup as one of the first employees and do work that directly impacts the future of how companies are built.

‍

👩‍💻👨‍💻 Role
We’re looking for the first growth engineer to join our team and obsess over how customers discover and experience Stable.

From early-stage startups to publicly traded companies, over 10,000+ businesses trust Stable to operate their mailroom, maintain their entity compliance, and automate their back-office workflows. 

We’re early, and we have ambitions to empower many more of the 33 million businesses based in the US. As the first growth engineer at Stable you will help us expand from thousands of customers to millions of customers: you will imagine, design, measure, and build software to increase the reach and impact of our product.

This role and team will evolve, but you will be responsible for helping it take shape. Here are a few examples of the types of projects we think you may work on:

Data Analysis + Experimentation – Analyze internal and external data to run experiments in domains like automated content generation, go-to-market motions, landing pages, and more.
Core Product – Contribute to core product onboarding flows and functionality to boost conversion, increase referral rates, and grow expansion revenue.
Targeted Outreach – Work directly with our go-to-market teams to automatically identify and target potential customers based on unique buying criteria and channels.
Marketing Website – Instrument, improve, and help manage our marketing assets i.e. useStable.com
This role is ideal for creative engineers invigorated by solving hard problems, thinking outside of the box, and moving fast.

‍

‍😀 What you value
Be human: Lead with empathy, act with authenticity, and enjoy what you do.
Stay curious: Invent novel solutions by asking why, listening, and tinkering.
Act quickly with purpose: Focus on what matters, iteratively improve, and move urgently towards the goal.
Insist on exceptional outcomes: Strive for excellence and take ownership over the outcomes you deliver.
Exceed customer expectations: Create delightful experiences with each interaction.
‍

✅ What You'll Do
Define how customers discover and experience Stable: from first interaction, to signup, to ongoing product usage the expectation is that you will consider and improve the entire customer lifecycle
Work directly with our go-to-market teams; use software to accelerate and expand the scope and impact of the projects our go-to-market teams can work on
Run data driven experiments, analyze results, and quickly implement decisions
You will be the first growth engineer at Stable and have the opportunity to create our long-term roadmap, build shared experimentation methodologies, and define how this function interfaces with the entire business
Technologies we use: MySQL, Webflow, Google Analytics, Segment, Metabase, Posthog, React, TypeScript, Node.js, GraphQL, AWS (if you think a different technology can solve problems better, we’re all ears)
‍

✨Requirements
3+ years experience as a full stack engineer
3+ years experience working on data or growth engineering
Experience with SQL and analytics tools like Posthog or Amplitude
Self-directed and comfortable leading projects
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain complex workflows, along with the big picture business context to other teams
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, high-growth environment, balancing quick wins with longer-term strategic thinking
Nice to have: Experience in startup environments or early-stage product work.
‍

🎁 What we offer
Competitive salary and generous equity 🚀
Unlimited paid time off 🏖
Medical, dental, and vision insurance 🏥
Home office set-up 🖥
Hybrid role, with regular visits to our Dallas processing center 💻🏢
Opportunities to shape the future of Stable and grow into leadership roles 💌

me and a coworker are both on the call together (we are both on the data team).

lets ideate and come up with some good questions to ask throughout the interview

The role is for a first growth engineer who will:
- Work on data analysis and experimentation
- Contribute to core product features for conversion/referral/expansion
- Work with go-to-market teams on targeted outreach
- Improve marketing website

Key requirements:
- 3+ years full stack engineering
- 3+ years data/growth engineering
- SQL and analytics tools experience

- Good communication skills

2. Assess technical skills around experimentation
3. Understand how the candidate thinks about growth
4. Evaluate collaboration abilities (especially with data team)

content.thinking: Let me start creating the interview questions organized by category. I'll make them relevant to Stable's business and the specific role requirements.

content.text:
## Data & Analytics Questions (Leverage your team's expertise)

**Experimentation & Metrics:**
- "Walk us through how you'd design an A/B test to improve our onboarding conversion rate. What metrics would you track beyond just the primary conversion metric?"
- "We notice a 20% drop in signups last week. How would you investigate this? What data would you look at first?"
- "How do you balance statistical significance with business urgency when running experiments?"

**SQL & Technical:**
- "How would you identify which customer segments have the highest expansion revenue potential using SQL?"
- "Describe a time when you had to optimize a slow query that was impacting a production dashboard or feature."

content.thinking: Good, now let me continue with growth strategy questions.

content.text:
## Growth Strategy Questions

**Product-Led Growth:**
- "How would you approach increasing referral rates for a B2B product like ours? Give us a specific example of something you'd test."
- "What's your framework for prioritizing growth initiatives when you have limited engineering resources?"
- "Tell us about a growth loop you've built or would build for a business service company."

**Customer Lifecycle:**
- "How would you identify and target businesses that are most likely to need a virtual mailbox service?"
- "What signals would you look for to identify customers ready to expand from basic mail handling to our compliance products?"

content.thinking: Now let me add collaboration questions since they'll work closely with the data team.

content.text:
## Collaboration & Cross-Functional Questions

**Working with Data Teams:**
- "How do you typically collaborate with data teams? Give an example of a successful project where data insights drove your engineering decisions."
- "What's your approach to instrumentation and making sure the data team has what they need for analysis?"
- "How do you balance building for quick experimentation versus building robust, scalable features?"

**Go-to-Market Partnership:**
- "Describe how you've worked with sales or marketing teams to build tools that accelerated their work."
- "How do you handle conflicting priorities between growth experiments and sales team feature requests?"

content.thinking: Finally, let me create some Stable-specific situational questions.

content.text:
## Stable-Specific Situational Questions

**Understanding the Business:**
- "Based on what you know about Stable, what growth opportunities excite you most and why?"
- "We have customers ranging from early startups to public companies. How would your growth strategies differ for these segments?"
- "Virtual mail is a new category for many businesses. How would you approach educating the market while driving conversions?"

**First Growth Engineer Challenges:**
- "As our first growth engineer, you'll need to build processes from scratch. What would be your 30-60-90 day plan?"
- "How would you establish a culture of experimentation when you're the only growth engineer?"
- "What growth infrastructure would you prioritize building first?"

## Red Flags to Watch For:
- Over-reliance on paid acquisition without organic growth thinking
- No experience with B2B or longer sales cycles
- Can't articulate how they measure experiment success/failure
- Poor SQL skills or unfamiliarity with analytics tools
- Lack of autonomy or needs heavy direction

## Green Flags:
- Specific examples of cross-functional wins with data/sales/marketing teams
- Clear framework for prioritization
- Experience with both quick wins and long-term platform building
- Comfortable with ambiguity and building from scratch
- Genuine curiosity about Stable's unique market position

content.text:
**Pro tip:** Since you're both from data team, lean into questions about metrics, instrumentation, and experimentation methodology. You can also ask follow-ups about statistical concepts or how they'd structure data for analysis - areas where you have deep expertise to properly evaluate their answers.

Good luck with the interview tomorrow! 🎯

content.text: I'll help you complete the "Getting to know the candidate" section with thoughtful questions that flow naturally and help you understand their background and motivations.

content.content: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable

content.text:
## Getting to Know the Candidate

**Your team introduction (to set context):**
- "We're on the data team here at Stable. We work closely with product and engineering to understand user behavior, measure feature impact, and provide insights that drive product decisions. As the first growth engineer, you'd be one of our closest partners - we'd provide you with analysis and insights, help you design experiments, and ensure we're tracking the right metrics. You'd help us by building the instrumentation we need and turning our insights into actual features and experiments."

**Getting to know you questions:**
- "Tell us a bit about your background and what attracted you to this growth engineering role at Stable."
- "What's a growth or data project you've worked on that you're particularly proud of? Walk us through your role and the impact."
- "How do you think about the balance between being data-driven and moving quickly? Have you ever had to make growth decisions with imperfect data?"
- "What excites you most about being the first growth engineer at a company versus joining an established growth team?"
- "Based on your understanding of Stable's business, what questions would you want to answer with data in your first few weeks?"

**Optional follow-ups based on their responses:**
- "You mentioned [specific tool/framework] - how did you decide that was the right approach?"
- "What did you learn from that experience that you'd apply here?"
- "How did you measure success for that initiative?"

content.text:
These questions help you:
1. Set expectations about collaboration with your data team
2. Understand their technical depth and growth mindset
3. Assess their comfort with ambiguity and building from scratch
4. Gauge their curiosity about Stable's specific business model
5. See how they think about measurement and impact

The flow feels natural and gives them multiple opportunities to showcase relevant experience while you evaluate their fit for this unique "first growth engineer" role.
